title: Per-row access control
scenario_context: Some rows visible to user, some not.
verification_chain: Layer 1: ACL matrix by role and resource
Layer 3: query filter applies to list/search/count/export
Layer 9: unauthorized rows and aggregate counts are absent
Layer 10: denied_access_total and filtered_count by route
reasoning: Server filters at query. Indexes match. Counts respect.
frontend_solution:
// Frontend contract for e2e-502: render the user-visible state, drive it through real interactions, and assert accessible output.
// Prefer role/name selectors, wait for network-visible state changes, and cover loading/error/empty/success paths without relying on implementation details.

error_handling_strategy.server_errors: 403 or 404 for unauthorized direct access; never partial hidden rows in totals
error_handling_strategy.client_error_mapping: PermissionDenied state with no leaked count
error_handling_strategy.user_facing_messages: You do not have access to this view
error_handling_strategy.recovery_actions: Switch account, request access, or clear stale filters

test_strategy: Seed owner/member/viewer/outsider roles across two tenants; assert list, count, search, export, pagination, and sub-resource endpoints never leak unauthorized rows or counts.
observability_hooks: Emit route, actorRole, resourceType, tenantId hash, denied reason, and filtered_count without logging protected row IDs.
